ctxMarket: Attention is on Solana’s price dropping below $76, its lowest since 2023, with some speculation about further declines. Meanwhile, Hyperliquid’s HYPE token has surpassed SOL in price, though not in market cap.
ctxTalking:
• Solana’s price drop and speculation about further downside
• Hyperliquid’s HYPE token overtaking SOL in price
• New Solana-native products: onchain perps trading and native subscriptions
ctxMatters: Solana’s declining price is drawing scrutiny and could affect sentiment among users and builders. Despite this, development continues with new trading platforms and payment primitives launching on Solana. These features may expand Solana’s use cases and ecosystem resilience.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Ongoing product launches and infrastructure upgrades on Solana, even during price volatility.
Noise: Short-term speculation about SOL’s price trajectory without clear catalysts.
ctxGlossary:
Perps — Perpetual futures contracts, allowing traders to speculate on asset prices without expiry.
Onchain — Activities or applications that operate directly on a blockchain, not off-chain.

readMore.title: Hyperliquid’s HYPE overtakes Solana in price as SOL falls to lowest levels since 2023
Solana Now Has Native Subscriptions & Allowances
